method: audit psi_calibrate_emit.hexa formula → port _suggest() identically into drift recomputation → diff cache vs. live-log p95+5 with op-clamp parity
problem_statement: ω-network-6b keeps the cache age-fresh (re-computed daily) but no tool detects when the underlying log distribution drifts from the cached calibration. The cache could be technically fresh (re-computed yesterday) yet semantically stale relative to today's host load.

drift_metric.recompute_formula: identical to psi_calibrate_emit.hexa _suggest(): rej_ratio>0.10 → round(p95+5) capped at 95 floored at current(80); else max<current-20 → round(max+10) floored at 30; else KEEP(80). Op-clamp ubu1/ubu2 ≤ 70.

verdict_logic: any host >30% OR new host in log not in cache OR cache mtime < log max_ts → __PSI_DRIFT__ STALE
preflight_skips: cache file missing → SKIP reason=cache_missing path=PATH
log file missing → SKIP reason=log_missing path=PATH
per-host samples<50 → INSUFFICIENT for that host (others still computed)

cli.default_output: human-readable per-host table HOST CACHED RECOMPUTED DRIFT_PCT VERDICT followed by sentinel

f2_small_drift.input: hetzner cache=70 vs log p95=75 → recomputed=80

f3_large_drift.input: hetzner cache=70 vs log p95=92 → recomputed=95 (cap)

f4_new_host_in_log.input: log has newhost rows, cache only HETZNER

f5_insufficient_samples.expected: ubu1 ok, hetzner INSUFFICIENT, sentinel FRESH (insufficient_hosts=1)

edge_cases_tested: cache containing only header + * default row (SKIP cache_empty)
real cache + real log (smoke: max_drift=0% but cache_mtime<log_max_ts → STALE — correct, expected behavior)
constraints_honored: no commit in anima (this closure JSON is UNTRACKED)
no parallel-session collision: cli/run.hexa, atlas timeline, atlas composite, n6/*, kick_*, design/kick/* all left untouched

exit_codes.2: defensive parse fault (unreachable in normal flow)

headline: drift detector closes the freshness gap: ω-network-6 keeps cache age-fresh; ω-network-8 keeps cache distribution-fresh by re-running the formula and flagging hosts whose load has shifted >10%/>30%.
